records.ety: [OE. bali, bely, AS. belg, bælg, bælig, bag, bellows, belly; akin to Icel. belgr bag, bellows, Sw. bälg, Dan. bælg, D. & G. balg, cf. W. bol the paunch or belly, dim. boly, Ir. bolg. Cf. Bellows, Follicle, Fool, Bilge.]

defs: That part of the human body which extends downward from the breast to the thighs, and contains the bowels, or intestines; the abdomen.
notes: ☞ Formerly all the splanchnic or visceral cavities were called bellies; -- the lower belly being the abdomen; the middle belly, the thorax; and the upper belly, the head.

defs: The under part of the body of animals, corresponding to the human belly.
quotes: Underneath the belly of their steeds.
Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee.

defs: The part of anything which resembles the human belly in protuberance or in cavity; the innermost part; as, the belly of a flask, muscle, sail, ship.
quotes: Out of the belly of hell cried I.

defs: The hollow part of a curved or bent timber, the convex part of which is the back.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bellied (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Bellying.]

defs: To cause to swell out; to fill.
quotes: Your breath of full consent bellied his sails.

defs: To swell and become protuberant, like the belly; to bulge.
quotes: The bellying canvas strutted with the gale.
